mnemonics.title: PASCAL Triangle
mnemonics.content: P - Previous row entries A - Add them together S - Sum gives current C - Current entry formed A - All rows start/end with 1 L - Like a triangle shape. Each number is sum of two numbers directly above it.

mnemonics.title: nCk Formula
mnemonics.content: nCk = n! / (k! × (n-k)!). Remember: TOP factorial divided by BOTTOM factorial times REMAINDER factorial.

mnemonics.title: 2^n Sum
mnemonics.content: Remember 'Two Naughty Kids': Σ C(n,k) = 2^n. Two for total subsets, Naughty for n, Kids for k.

acronyms.usage: Used to count selections without order. Read as 'n choose k'.

acronyms.expansion: Pascal's Triangle Identity: A S C - Add Summed Current
acronyms.usage: Each entry = Sum of entries Above-left and Above-right in Pascal's Triangle

visualTips.visualization: Draw triangle with 1s on both edges. Each interior number fills gap between two numbers above it by adding them. Row 0: 1; Row 1: 1 1; Row 2: 1 2 1; Row 3: 1 3 3 1; Row 4: 1 4 6 4 1.
Imagine mirror at center of each row. C(n,k) mirrors to C(n,n-k). Row 5: 1-5-10-10-5-1 is symmetric.
Think of (x+y)^n as selecting x k times and y (n-k) times. Coefficient = ways to choose which positions get x.

analogies.analogy: Like choosing committee members from a group. C(10,3) = ways to form a 3-person committee from 10 people. Order doesn't matter - just who's on committee.
Each of n items can be either in or out of a subset. Total subsets = 2×2×2... n times = 2^n. C(n,k) counts subsets of size k.
Like counting paths in a grid. To reach position (n,k), you either came from (n-1,k-1) or (n-1,k). Total ways = sum of both paths.
